plan.reasoning: SMILES consistency check: the adsorbate is [N]=[NH], i.e. NNH/diimide radical-like species with two heavy atoms. The provided atom list is internally consistent with this SMILES: atom 0 is N, SP2, degree 1, radical_electrons 1; atom 1 is N, SP2, degree 1, radical_electrons 0. There is no mismatch between requested adsorbate and SMILES. Because I will bind through atom 0, which explicitly has one unpaired electron, classifying this as ReactiveSpecies is chemically valid. No history exists, so no prior sites have been tested and there are no unstable-site warnings or convergence tags to respect. The user wants the lowest-energy adsorption configuration for NNH on a Cu3Ag(111) alloy surface. For N-containing radicals on coinage-metal alloy surfaces, the most chemically plausible strongest initial anchoring motif is end-on binding through the terminal radical N atom rather than through the protonated NH terminus. Hollow sites are often too highly coordinated for a small end-on N-centered radical as an initial guess, while bridge can be competitive but usually after establishing whether the stronger localized N-metal sigma interaction prefers a single metal atom. On a heterogeneous Cu-Ag surface, Cu is expected to bind N-containing adsorbates more strongly than Ag because Cu is generally more reactive than Ag. Therefore the best first and only allowed systematic initial plan is terminal N adsorption on a Cu ontop site. This uses the correct binding atom index from the provided list: atom 0. Site naming must use the canonical label 'ontop'.
